pattern: Simulate one pass of the instructions; the robot is bounded exactly when it returns to the origin or ends up facing a direction other than its start.
hints: Run the instruction string once and track the final position and heading.
If after one cycle the robot is back at the origin, it obviously loops; the subtler case is when it ends facing a different direction.
Any net rotation means the displacement vector rotates each cycle and the four cycles cancel, so the robot is bounded unless it both returns to no rotation and has moved away from the origin.

edge_inputs.description: single left turn keeps it bounded
only turns, never moves, stays at origin
net rotation but ends off origin
four right turns net to north again but displaced

stress.description: random instruction strings of varying length
